title: PRINCIPLES AND GUIDELINES FOR THE ASSESSMENT AND USE OF VOLUNTARY THIRD-PARTY ASSURANCE PROGRAMMES CXG 93-2021

sections.section: PRINCIPLES AND GUIDELINES FOR THE ASSESSMENT AND USE OF VOLUNTARY THIRD-PARTY ASSURANCE PROGRAMMES CXG 93-2021
sections.summary: Document title: Principles and Guidelines for the Assessment and Use of Voluntary Third-Party Assurance Programmes (CXG 93-2021), adopted in 2021.

Preamble states that food business operators (FBOs) have primary responsibility for food safety and regulatory compliance, competent authorities may use vTPA programme information/data if they ensure it is reliable and fit for purpose, the guidelines provide a framework and criteria to assess vTPA governance and data, and reliable vTPA data can support risk-based prioritisation of official controls while poorly performing programmes may lead to increased regulatory controls.

Scope: guidelines assist competent authorities within national boundaries to assess and transparently use vTPA programme information/data to support NFCS objectives, focus on vTPA structure/governance/components, are voluntary and do not apply to official government inspection/certification systems or private commercial standards, and do not constitute approval or endorsement of vTPA programmes.

Provides definitions for terms used in the document—such as assessment, accreditation, accreditation body, audit, certification body, conformity assessment, governance, inspection, review, specified requirement, vTPA standard, Voluntary Third-Party Assurance Programme and vTPA owner—many adapted from ISO/IEC and Codex sources.
Principle 6: competent authorities should ensure confidentiality of information/data shared by vTPA owners in line with relevant national legal requirements.

sections.section: Principle 7 Avoiding burdens on food business operators
sections.summary: Principle 7: processes and policies for competent authorities' use of vTPA information/data should not impose additional requirements, costs or restrictions on FBOs beyond regulatory requirements.

sections.section: E. ROLES, RESPONSIBILITIES AND RELEVANT ACTIVITIES
sections.summary: Roles and responsibilities: taking account of reliable vTPA information/data in the NFCS should not change the roles and responsibilities of participants along the food chain.

Competent authorities have statutory NFCS responsibilities, may use vTPA data to support NFCS objectives, must apply regulatory controls to all FBOs regardless of vTPA participation, should clearly describe and transparently implement any use of vTPA information/data, verify ongoing credibility and reliability, recognize conflicts of interest, and maintain confidentiality of shared data according to national law.

Food Business Operators (FBOs) retain primary responsibility for food safety and regulatory compliance, must demonstrate effective controls, may voluntarily participate in vTPA programmes to demonstrate conformity and provide assurance, own the information/data generated by the vTPA, and should not have conflicts of interest with the vTPA operation.

vTPA owners are responsible for programme governance (which may include national/international standards and accredited audit), must disclose potential data-sharing with competent authorities, have mechanisms and policies to share information and notify FBOs, must alert authorities to significant public-health risks or consumer deception from FBO non-conformities, and must manage conflicts of interest and demonstrate adherence to data protection obligations.

sections.section: F. CRITERIA TO ASSESS THE CREDIBILITY AND INTEGRITY OF vTPA PROGRAMMES
sections.summary: Competent authorities wishing to use vTPA programme information/data must ensure it is trustworthy and fit for purpose and may carry out a full or partial, commensurate assessment of the programme’s credibility and integrity using the listed criteria.

vTPA programmes should have clearly documented governance arrangements and responsibilities, oversight to avoid conflicts of interest, management controls for consistent implementation, and either accreditation by a reputable accreditation body or alternative measures to ensure certification bodies’ competency.

The vTPA programme must ensure certification bodies are appropriately accredited to the relevant accreditation standard via an independent process, with accreditation assessed against internationally recognized standards and subject to periodic review and renewal.

vTPA standards should be either owner-set or based on national/international standards, be consistent with Codex or applicable regulations, include consumer protection (food safety and fair trade) requirements, be developed transparently with stakeholders, be open to regular review, and be written so conformity can be assessed.

The vTPA programme must have documented conformity assessment procedures covering audit frequency and methodology (including announced and unannounced audits), defined periodic assessments (e.g., annual audits), auditor competence requirements, and a transparent system to identify FBOs that conform to the standard.

vTPA governance must include clear procedures for addressing non‑conformities and failures to implement corrective actions, including potential sanctions such as certification withdrawal, review of audit reports and decisions, and an appeals procedure.

vTPA owners should provide up‑to‑date lists of certified/verified FBOs (and status) accessible to competent authorities (and possibly publicly), notify authorities promptly of significant public health risks or participation changes subject to data protection, have lawful permission and protocols for data sharing and retention, secure electronic data appropriately, notify authorities of programme changes, and share conformity data where aligned with regulatory requirements.

sections.section: ## G. REGULATORY APPROACHES FOR THE USE OF vTPA PROGRAMME INFORMATION/DATA
sections.summary: Section heading introducing regulatory approaches for competent authorities’ use of vTPA programme information/data (no substantive requirements in the heading itself).

sections.section: 14. This  section  provides  examples  of  process  considerations  and  policy  options  available  for  competent authorities  when  they  establish  arrangements  with  vTPA  owners  to  use  information/data  from  vTPA programmes. It also contains examples of the practical uses that can be made by competent authorities of vTPA programme information/data to support their NFCS objectives.
sections.summary: This section offers examples of process considerations and policy options for competent authorities when establishing arrangements to use vTPA programme information/data and examples of practical uses to support NFCS objectives.

Competent authorities should assess vTPA credibility commensurate with intended use, may enter mutual arrangements requiring data sharing and non‑conformity handling, establish verification procedures and regular communication (including trend analysis and audit comparisons), exchange routine governance information or obtain data directly from FBOs, and agree access arrangements for the audit elements most valuable to NFCS objectives.
